questions.prompt: The Fourier series represents a periodic signal as:
choices: A. A single sinusoid at the fundamental frequency
B. A sum of sinusoids at integer multiples (harmonics) of the fundamental frequency
D. A product of cosine functions
questions.explanation: Fourier series: f(t) = a‚āÄ + ő£[a‚āôcos(nŌČ‚āÄt) + b‚āôsin(nŌČ‚āÄt)]. Decomposes a periodic signal into its harmonic content.

questions.prompt: The fundamental frequency ŌČ‚āÄ for a signal with period T is:
questions.explanation: ŌČ‚āÄ = 2ŌÄ/T rad/s; f‚āÄ = 1/T Hz. The fundamental period and frequency are reciprocals (up to 2ŌÄ factor).

questions.prompt: For an even function f(t) = f(‚ąít), the Fourier series contains:
choices: B. Only cosine terms (and DC term a‚āÄ)
C. Both sine and cosine terms
questions.explanation: Even symmetry ‚Üí b‚āô = 0 for all n. Only cosine (and DC) terms. Odd function f(‚ąít) = ‚ąíf(t) ‚Üí a‚āô = 0, only sine terms.

questions.prompt: For a signal with half-wave symmetry f(t + T/2) = ‚ąíf(t), the Fourier series contains:
choices: D. Both even and odd harmonics
questions.explanation: Half-wave symmetry: only odd harmonics (n = 1, 3, 5, ‚Ä¶). No DC term, no even harmonics.

questions.prompt: The DC component a‚āÄ in a Fourier series equals:
choices: A. The average value of f(t) over one period
questions.explanation: a‚āÄ = (1/T) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ f(t) dt ‚ÄĒ the average (mean) value of the function over one period.

questions.prompt: The Fourier coefficients a‚āô are computed by:
questions.explanation: a‚āô = (2/T) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ f(t) cos(nŌČ‚āÄt) dt for n ‚Č• 1. Uses orthogonality of cosine functions.

questions.prompt: Parseval's theorem states that for a periodic signal:
choices: B. Total energy = integral of f(t)
questions.explanation: Parseval's: <f¬≤> = a‚āÄ¬≤ + ¬Ĺő£(a‚āô¬≤ + b‚āô¬≤). Power in time domain = sum of power in each harmonic.

questions.prompt: A square wave with 50% duty cycle and amplitude ¬ĪA has a Fourier series with:
choices: B. No DC, only odd harmonics with amplitude 4A/(nŌÄ)
C. All harmonics with equal amplitude
questions.explanation: Square wave (zero mean, ¬ĪA): f(t) = (4A/ŌÄ)[sin(ŌČ‚āÄt) + sin(3ŌČ‚āÄt)/3 + sin(5ŌČ‚āÄt)/5 + ‚Ä¶]. Odd harmonics, decreasing as 1/n.

questions.prompt: The exponential form of the Fourier series uses complex coefficients c‚āô = ?
questions.explanation: Exponential FS: f(t) = ő£c‚āô e^(jnŌČ‚āÄt); c‚āô = (1/T)‚ąęf(t)e^(‚ąíjnŌČ‚āÄt)dt. Related: c‚āô = (a‚āô‚ąíjb‚āô)/2 for n>0.

questions.prompt: A triangular wave has harmonics that decrease as:
questions.explanation: Triangular wave: amplitude ‚ąĚ 1/n¬≤. Square wave: 1/n. Smoothness ‚Üí faster harmonic decay (per integration theorem).

questions.prompt: The Gibbs phenomenon occurs in Fourier series approximations at:
choices: A. Smooth portions of the signal
B. Discontinuities, where the partial sum overshoots by ~9% of the jump
questions.explanation: Gibbs phenomenon: at jump discontinuities, the Fourier series overshoots ~8.9% of the jump regardless of how many terms are used.

questions.prompt: Filtering a signal by passing it through a linear time-invariant (LTI) system multiplies the Fourier coefficients by:
choices: A. The system's impulse response h(t)
B. The system's frequency response H(nŌČ‚āÄ) at each harmonic
C. A constant factor for all harmonics
D. Nothing ‚ÄĒ the Fourier coefficients are unchanged
questions.explanation: LTI filtering in frequency domain: output c‚āô_out = c‚āô_in √ó H(nŌČ‚āÄ). Each harmonic is multiplied by H at that frequency.

questions.prompt: The Fourier series converges to the midpoint of a jump discontinuity because of the:
choices: B. Dirichlet conditions and Fourier convergence theorem
questions.explanation: Dirichlet conditions: at a jump, FS converges to the average of left and right limits: [f(t+) + f(t‚ąí)] / 2.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a Dirichlet condition for Fourier series convergence?
choices: A. f(t) must be differentiable everywhere
B. f(t) must be absolutely integrable over one period: ‚ąę|f(t)|dt < ‚ąě
C. f(t) must be an even function
D. f(t) must be continuous everywhere
questions.explanation: Dirichlet conditions: f(t) single-valued, finite discontinuities, bounded variation, and absolutely integrable. Continuity is not required.

questions.prompt: The Fourier series is useful in which applications? (Select ALL that apply.)
choices: A. Analyzing steady-state response to periodic inputs
C. Computing transient response of linear systems
D. Analyzing audio and speech signals
questions.explanation: Fourier series is for periodic signals: steady-state response, power harmonics, audio analysis. Transient (non-periodic) response uses Fourier/Laplace transforms.

questions.prompt: A sawtooth wave from 0 to 2ŌÄ has fundamental amplitude (b‚āĀ) = 2/1 = 2. What is the 3rd harmonic amplitude b‚āÉ?
questions.explanation: Sawtooth: b‚āô = 2/n (with appropriate normalization). b‚āÉ = 2/3 ‚Čą 0.667

questions.prompt: A periodic signal of period T = 2 ms has a fundamental frequency of:
The Fourier transform is an extension of the Fourier series for:
choices: B. Aperiodic (non-periodic) signals with finite energy
D. Signals with no DC component
questions.explanation: Fourier transform generalizes FS to aperiodic signals by letting T ‚Üí ‚ąě, converting the discrete spectrum to a continuous one.

questions.prompt: The frequency spectrum of a periodic signal with fundamental f‚āÄ has spectral lines at:
questions.explanation: Periodic signal: discrete spectrum at DC (0), f‚āÄ, 2f‚āÄ, 3f‚āÄ, ‚Ä¶ The spacing is f‚āÄ.

questions.prompt: The time-shift property of Fourier series states that shifting f(t) by t‚āÄ multiplies c‚āô by:
questions.explanation: Time shift: f(t‚ąít‚āÄ) ‚ÜĒ c‚āô e^(‚ąíjnŌČ‚āÄt‚āÄ). Only phase changes; magnitudes |c‚āô| remain unchanged.

questions.prompt: Multiplying a Fourier series by a constant K multiplies:
choices: A. The fundamental frequency by K
B. All Fourier coefficients by K
questions.explanation: Linearity: K¬∑f(t) ‚ÜĒ K¬∑c‚āô for all n. The period and frequency are unchanged.

questions.prompt: A square wave with period T = 1 ms and amplitude A = 5 V (zero mean). The fundamental (1st harmonic) peak amplitude = 4A/ŌÄ ‚Čą ?
questions.explanation: 1st harmonic peak = 4A/ŌÄ = 4√ó5/ŌÄ = 20/ŌÄ ‚Čą 6.37 V

questions.prompt: Total harmonic distortion (THD) is computed from Fourier series as:
choices: A. THD = ‚ąö(ő£ A‚āô¬≤ for n‚Č•2) / A‚āĀ
questions.explanation: THD = ‚ąö(A‚āā¬≤+A‚āÉ¬≤+‚Ä¶) / A‚āĀ. Measures harmonic content relative to fundamental. Important in power systems and audio.

questions.prompt: A full-wave rectified sine wave has fundamental frequency _____ the original sine wave.
questions.explanation: Full-wave rectification folds negative half-cycles up ‚Üí period is halved ‚Üí fundamental frequency doubles to 2f‚āÄ.

questions.prompt: For an odd function f(t) = -f(-t), the trigonometric Fourier series contains:
choices: D. Both sine and cosine terms with equal coefficients
questions.explanation: Odd symmetry forces a0=0 and an=0, leaving only sine coefficients bn.
